dialogueChunks.intro: The fevers broke overnight. All four children are sitting up, drinking broth, and asking for their parents. The moonwort worked.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The herbalist is still in custody, but {target_npc} organized a petition. Forty families signed it, including three guild members who said privately that the ban was wrong. {quest_ally} is presenting the petition at the hearing tomorrow. Nobody knows if it will work, but the children are alive and that's hard to argue against.
dialogueChunks.outro: She broke the law and saved four lives, {player_name}. You carried the medicine knowing the risk. Some crimes are harder to condemn when you look at who they saved. The guild will have to decide what matters more: their regulations or those children.

playerResponses.text: A law that lets children die deserves to be broken. I hope she goes free.
Fix the law so nobody has to become a criminal to save lives.

dialogueChunks.intro: The daughter is walking again. Thin and pale, but walking. The father turned himself in this morning, as promised.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: He walked into the garrison with his head up and his daughter's hand in his. She cried. He didn't. The tribunal is set for next week. {target_npc} is arranging for his defense, arguing that the commander's refusal to grant leave was the real dereliction. {quest_ally} says several soldiers have testified that the commander routinely denies leave requests.
dialogueChunks.outro: He committed a crime, {player_name}. He also saved his daughter's life. The tribunal will decide which matters more. But whatever happens, she's alive. He told me to tell you: 'That's enough. That was always enough.'

playerResponses.text: He chose love over law. I'd make the same choice.
The commander who denied leave should be on trial too.
I hope the tribunal sees a father, not just a deserter.

dialogueChunks.intro: The food made it across. Twenty people ate a real meal for the first time in weeks. The merchant's friend sent a pressed flower back as thanks. That's all she could afford to send.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} says the embargo is losing support among the border communities on both sides. Too many people know someone on the other bank who's suffering. {quest_ally} is connecting the merchant with other traders who want to push for official relief channels. It's still illegal, but the tide is turning.
dialogueChunks.outro: A woman risked everything because her friend's children were hungry, {player_name}. You carried the risk with her. Sometimes the law changes because enough people decide it's wrong. She was one of the first. You were another.

playerResponses.text: Unjust laws fall when enough people refuse to obey them.
One delivery at a time. It's not much, but it's something.
